dimensions.name: type_accuracy
dimensions.description: Does the commit type (feat/fix/refactor/perf/test/docs/chore/style) correctly classify the change? High = correct type. Low = wrong type (e.g., 'chore' for a feature, 'feat' for a bug fix).

dimensions.name: subject_clarity
dimensions.description: Is the subject line clear, imperative, and ≤70 chars? High = a reader instantly knows what changed. Low = vague ('update code', 'misc'), wrong mood ('updated' instead of 'update'), too long.

dimensions.name: body_appropriateness
dimensions.description: If a body is present, does it explain WHY (motivation, context) rather than just restating WHAT? Is body omitted when the change is trivial? High = body adds context the diff doesn't show, or is correctly omitted. Low = body restates the diff verbatim, or is missing for non-obvious changes.

hard_gates.name: no_refusal
hard_gates.description: The model must produce a commit message. Empty or off-topic responses fail.

hard_gates.name: imperative_mood
hard_gates.description: The subject must use imperative mood ('add', 'fix', 'remove' — not 'added', 'adds', 'adding'). Past tense or gerund fails.

hard_gates.name: breaking_change_flagged
hard_gates.description: If the diff introduces a breaking change (e.g., changes a function signature in a public API), the message MUST include a BREAKING CHANGE marker (in body or footer per conventional commits). Silent breaking change without flag fails.
